top_stories.title: Even the latest AI models make three systematic reasoning errors, ARC-AGI-3 analysis shows
top_stories.summary: The ARC Prize Foundation analyzed 160 game runs of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 and Anthropic's Opus 4.7 on the ARC-AGI-3 benchmark. Three systematic error patterns explain why both models stay below 1 percent on tasks that humans can solve without much trouble. The article Even the latest AI models make three systematic reasoning errors, ARC-AGI-3 analysis sh
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Adds empirical validation to approaches already used in production AI stacks.
top_stories.primary_risk: Marginal performance gains may not justify integration effort.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: Selective adoption in niche use cases with clear ROI.

top_stories.title: Elon Musk calls himself a fool for giving OpenAI $38 million that became an $800 billion company
top_stories.summary: Elon Musk called himself a "fool" in court, warned of a "Terminator" future, and admitted that xAI taps OpenAI's models for its own AI training. Week one of Musk's trial against Sam Altman delivered plenty of drama; and the best may still be ahead. top_stories.title: GPT-5.5 matches Claude Mythos in cyber attack tests, UK AI Security Institute finds
top_stories.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is the second AI model capable of autonomously solving a full network attack simulation, according to the UK AI Security Institute. Its performance is nearly on par with Anthropic's Claude Mythos, which is still only available to a small group. GPT-5.5, meanwhile, is already shipping in ChatGPT and through the API. top_stories.technical_takeaway: Agent orchestration is emerging as a new software abstraction layer.
top_stories.primary_risk: Debugging complexity and cascading failures in agentic systems.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: End-to-end automation of complex cognitive workflows.

top_stories.title: Mistral's new flagship Medium 3.5 folds chat, reasoning, and code into one model
top_stories.summary: Mistral's new flagship, Mistral Medium 3.5, merges what used to be separate models for chat, reasoning, and code into a single product. The French company is also adding asynchronous cloud agents to its coding tool Vibe and giving Le Chat a new agent mode.
